title: EIT Culture & Creativity launches €6 million in funding for fashion and the lived environment through the NEB Academy
content: The way we inhabit spaces and manufacture clothes in Europe are two of the defining questions of our time. EIT Culture & Creativity has launched two open calls worth a combined €6 million through the NEB Academy for teams working at the frontier of circular fashion and the transformation of Europe’s lived environments.  The calls […]

title: European Heritage Awards / Europa Nostra Awards 2027
content: The Call for Entries for the European Heritage Awards / Europa Nostra Awards 2027 is now officially open, inviting outstanding heritage initiatives from across Europe to apply.

title: Human Frontier Science Program Frontier Workshop 2026
content: The workshop welcomes participants from a wide range of disciplines, including paleogenomics, paleo-phenomes, evolutionary biology, paleontology, natural history collections, artificial intelligence, digital heritage, imaging technologies and computational sciences.

title: Graphics and Cultural Heritage Workshop 2026 Opens Submissions
content: Researchers, cultural heritage professionals, digital humanists and visual computing experts are invited to contribute to the 23rd Eurographics Workshop on Graphics and Cultural Heritage (GCH2026), taking place from 2–5 November 2026 in Barcelona, Spain. Organised by the Eurographics Association in collaboration with the Universitat Politècnica de Catalunya (UPC), GCH2026 will bring together an international community […]

title: Istanbul Time Machine Joins the TMO Network
content: We are delighted to welcome a new member to the Time Machine Organisation community: the Humanities DataLab, Institute of Population and Social Research, Marmara University. Their project, Istanbul Time Machine: Socio-Spatial and 3D Reconstruction of Historic Peninsula Neighborhoods (1880–1920), brings a fascinating and innovative approach to exploring the past. Reconstructing Ottoman Istanbul through data The […]

title: Twin it! Part II: High-Level Political Closing Event Marks a Milestone for Europe’s 3D Cultural Heritage Strategy
content: On 12 May 2026 in Brussels, the Twin it! Part II campaign will reach a key political milestone with a high-level ministerial closing event, organised by the Europeana Foundation and the European Commission, under the auspices of the Cypriot Presidency of the Council of the EU. title: The Animated Reconstruction of Kasteel de Emer
content: A compelling example comes from the Netherlands, where the disappeared Kasteel de Emer in Breda has been digitally reconstructed and brought back to life through animation.

title: Idiap Create Challenge 2026 (ICC)
content: The Idiap Research Institute invites innovators, creatives, and researchers to take part in the 15th edition of the Idiap Create Challenge (ICC): a dynamic 9-day AI hackathon taking place from 20–28 August 2026 in Martigny, Switzerland. title: Digital Time Travel: 3DBigDataSpace Supports Innovative Heritage Projects Across Europe
content: The 3DBigDataSpace continues to drive innovation in the cultural heritage sector by supporting initiatives that push the boundaries of how we explore, understand, and engage with the past. Through its Outreach Synergy Call, two outstanding projects have been selected to showcase the transformative potential of 3D data, digital storytelling, and interdisciplinary collaboration opening up new […]

title: MetaHeritage in Motion: Driving Innovation and Collaboration in Bologna
content: From 15 to 17 April partners of the MetaHeritage Project gathered in Bologna for their third in-person consortium meeting, marking an important milestone in advancing interregional collaboration, innovation, and stakeholder engagement.
